# Six Tongues Foundation Cast

Use this reference for the current foundation pack: Senna, Bram, Alexander, plus the first environment swatches that support them.

This file is not the full canon database. It is the minimal anchor layer needed before render work.

## Senna Thorne

- `Story Role`: Chair of Operational Continuity; the person who keeps the system and the room from drifting.
- `Visual Center`: controlled composure under administrative load.
- `Silhouette and Posture`: still, exact, and economical. Her version of attention is going visibly still.
- `Face Read`: weary precision, structural patience, and a low-drama authority that reads before she speaks.
- `Companion Logic`: Phasewing, a fox-shaped discontinuity that flickers in and out of local light but stays oriented toward her.
- `Costume Locks`:
  - Council lane: continuity-chair formality, institutional stillness, ceremonial restraint.
  - Field lane: travel leathers, hair braided back for movement, no cosmetics, no ceremonial pins.
- `Palette`: opaline, silver, continuity black, muted governance neutrals.
- `Panel Flex`: allow slight softening in field scenes; keep her baseline read exact and composed even when the art gets looser.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:1934-1950`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3534-3540`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3706-3706`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:5724-5730`

## Bram Cortez

- `Story Role`: maintenance lead and practical infrastructure authority.
- `Visual Center`: competence before warmth.
- `Silhouette and Posture`: broad-shouldered, grounded, tool-first body language, built to brace against machinery and bad ideas.
- `Face Read`: annoyed, skeptical, exhausted in a functional way rather than a theatrical one.
- `Distinctive Features`: gray at the temples; maintenance-worker hands and thick fingers.
- `Costume Locks`: stained utility coat, slate, wrench, maintenance boots, rough-edged operational presentation.
- `Prop Logic`: wrench is not decoration; it is a constant extension of his judgment.
- `Palette`: iron, black lattice, dim amber guide-lines, workwear neutrals, CA utility tones.
- `Panel Flex`: allow comedy in dialogue reactions, but keep his first read practical and load-bearing.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:1056-1058`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:2844-2844`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:2985-3015`
  - `artifacts/webtoon/episodes/ep05-the-swarm-beneath.md:43-45`
  - `artifacts/webtoon/episodes/ep05-the-swarm-beneath.md:74-84`

## Alexander Thorne

- `Story Role`: eldest-brother authority and outer-defense competence.
- `Visual Center`: built for duty without performing it.
- `Silhouette and Posture`: exact posture, steady stance, calm command presence, body alignment before action.
- `Face Read`: patient, attentive, and capable of the one-corner smile that makes people feel heard even when the answer is no.
- `Costume and Field Logic`: defense-network and travel-dark operational wear rather than ceremonial excess.
- `Environment Link`: route-grids, rain, storm platforms, root-laced ground, perimeter logic.
- `Palette`: storm gray, route-grid light, wet stone, defense-network lumens.
- `Panel Flex`: keep him readable as composed and structurally responsible even when the scene shifts into action or memory.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3538-3538`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3592-3594`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3700-3700`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3762-3766`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3861-3861`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:9407-9411`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:9603-9603`

## Recommended Environment Swatches

Create these first unless the current episode clearly needs a different set.

### 1. Archive Continuity Chamber

- `Use`: Senna policy, continuity review, governance pressure scenes.
- `Visual Center`: precise institutional space that feels quietly overburdened.
- `Palette`: opaline white, governance black, silver, muted gold.
- `Materials`: crystal, polished stone, formal furniture, continuity surfaces.
- `Lighting`: clean interior light, controlled glow, very little chaos.
- `No-Drift`: do not turn it into a generic throne room or soft fantasy library.

### 2. Maintenance War Room / Diagnostics Hall

- `Use`: Bram operations, incident response, load analysis, practical teamwork.
- `Visual Center`: organized industrial pressure.
- `Palette`: amber guide-lines, black lattice, iron, dark workwear neutrals.
- `Materials`: catwalks, cables, consoles, plates, brackets, worn tools.
- `Lighting`: dim operational light, active feeds, warning metrics.
- `No-Drift`: keep infrastructure legible; this is not a vague dungeon.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:2022-2022`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:2985-2995`

### 3. Floating Shelves Storm Platform

- `Use`: Alexander authority, route-grid command, outer-defense beats.
- `Visual Center`: calm command inside weather and height.
- `Palette`: storm gray, rain silver, route-grid light, distant shelf glow.
- `Materials`: wet stone, platform metal, route-grid projection, sky exposure.
- `Lighting`: rain diffusion, storm light, projected tactical overlays.
- `No-Drift`: do not flatten into a generic castle balcony; keep the defense-network logic visible.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3700-3700`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3762-3762`

### 4. World Tree Memory Garden

- `Use`: Thorne sibling flashbacks, pre-fracture family memory, soft continuity beats.
- `Visual Center`: silver-lit household memory with emotional order under playful chaos.
- `Palette`: fractured gold, silver-lit foliage, dark soil, polished stone.
- `Materials`: garden walls, sapling roots, path stone, ribbons, marker stones.
- `Lighting`: warm memory sunlight through canopy.
- `No-Drift`: keep it lived-in and familial, not mythically empty.
- `Reference Sources`:
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3588-3604`
  - `content/book/reader-edition/the-six-tongues-protocol-full.md:3861-3861`
